title: Comparisons
description: Compare two values to see if they are equal or different.

slides.title: Comparison Operators ⚖️
slides.content:
Comparison operators are used to compare two values (or variables).

The return value of a comparison is either `true` (1) or `false` (0). This is incredibly useful for decision-making in your code.

slides.title: Equal To ==
slides.content:
To check if two values are equal, use the double equals `==` operator.

Be careful: A single `=` is for assignment. A double `==` is for comparison!

quizData.question: Using a single = inside an if condition (like if (x = 5)) checks if they are equal.
quizData.explanation: False! A single = assigns the value 5 to x. You MUST use == to check for equality.

slides.title: Other Comparisons 📏
slides.content:
Here are the other comparison operators:

* `!=` Not equal
* `>` Greater than
* `<` Less than
* `>=` Greater than or equal to
* `<=` Less than or equal to

quizData.question: What is the output of cout << (10 != 10); ?
quizData.explanation: Since 10 is equal to 10, asking if they are NOT equal (!=) results in false (0).
